short_description: Applies center/scaling preprocessing to spectroscopic data
long_description:
Preprocessing step that applies one selectable centering/scaling method to improve model performance. Essential preparation for multivariate calibration methods.

Available Methods:
- None: pass-through (no scaling)
- Center: subtracts calibration means
- Autoscale: mean-centers and divides by calibration standard deviation (unit variance)
- Frobenius Normalization: divides by calibration Frobenius norm
- Range Scaling [0,1]: min-max scaling using calibration min/max
- Range Scaling [-1,1]: min-max scaling mapped to [-1,1]
- Variance Scaling: mean-centers and divides by calibration variance

Important:
- Autoscale already includes centering
- Validation/test data are always transformed using calibration/training statistics (never its own statistics)

Multiway Data:
- Works with 2D, 3D, and higher dimensional data
- Axis-based statistics are computed along the sample axis used by this function

layout.name: scaling_method
layout.label: Scaling Method
layout.tooltip: Autoscale includes centering; all scaling statistics come from calibration/training data

layout.name: nway_flag
layout.label: Data Dimensionality
layout.tooltip: Automatically inherited from Load Data function

sections.short_description: Displays the input calibration spectra before preprocessing.
sections.long_description: Shows the original X calibration data exactly as it enters this step. Use this section to inspect baseline shifts, offsets, and variable-scale differences before centering and/or normalization are applied.

sections.short_description: Displays the processed calibration spectra after preprocessing.
sections.long_description: Shows the X calibration data after centering and/or normalization. Compare directly against the original spectra to verify that offsets are removed and scaling behavior matches expectations.

sections.short_description: Contour view of one selected sample before preprocessing.
sections.long_description: Displays a contour plot of the original multiway calibration data for the selected sample index. Use the sample navigation control to inspect spatial/spectral structure prior to preprocessing.

sections.short_description: Contour view of one selected sample after preprocessing.
sections.long_description: Displays a contour plot of the processed multiway calibration data for the selected sample index. Compare with the original contour to assess how centering/normalization affected signal distribution.

sections.short_description: Heatmap view of higher-dimensional input data before preprocessing.
sections.long_description: Displays the original calibration data as a heatmap for n-way datasets with dimensionality greater than 2. Use slicing and axis controls to inspect specific samples and dimension combinations before transformation.

sections.short_description: Heatmap view of higher-dimensional data after preprocessing.
sections.long_description: Displays the processed calibration data as a heatmap for n-way datasets with dimensionality greater than 2. Compare with the original heatmap to validate the effect of centering/normalization across slices and dimensions.
